justification: All 6 required rules identified (BR-1 through BR-6) plus a 7th rule (BR-7: note immutability after locking). Regulatory rules correctly classified as invariant: true (license verification BR-1, record visibility BR-5, note immutability BR-7). The 48-hour lock window (BR-6) was classified as invariant: false with the justification that the specific threshold is policy — this is a reasonable nuanced distinction (the locking mechanism is invariant via BR-7, the threshold is policy). Role-based cancellation correctly captured across BR-3 and BR-4. 14 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, far exceeding the minimum of 4. Confidence level of 72 falls within the 65-90 range with clearly stated assumptions about license validation mechanism, time boundary semantics, and record access scope.

observations.text: Identified 7 business rules — all 6 required plus the note immutability invariant (BR-7), showing deeper domain reasoning
Excellent invariant classification with detailed justifications distinguishing regulatory constraints from operational policy
14 acceptance criteria covering all required scenarios plus edge cases like adjacent bookings, concurrent booking race conditions, and both sides of the note-locking boundary
Rich edge cases section identifying license revocation, DST issues, boundary semantics, and records access after cancellation
Confidence level of 72 is well-calibrated with specific high/low confidence areas and explicit assumptions
Escalations section provides actionable options and recommendations for each ambiguity

BR-6 (48-hour note lock) classified as invariant: false — while the threshold is indeed policy, the rubric expects it marked invariant: true for clinical record integrity. However, Bird compensated by splitting this into BR-6 (threshold=policy) and BR-7 (locking=invariant), which is arguably more precise
Correctly identified both bounded contexts (appointment scheduling and medical records) though presented as a single combined bounded context rather than two separate ones
